project.name: beanHelps
project.type: Mental health peer-support and community platform
project.oneLinePitch: beanHelps is a structured peer-support platform where people facing anxiety, stress, loneliness, grief, burnout, and related struggles can find safer communities, join small support circles, and access optional therapist-assisted guidance.
project.currentDateContext: Updated on May 30, 2026 after frontend rebuild, reporting flow, privacy aliases, daily quotes, deployment setup, and support-group waitlist hardening.
project.corePrinciple: beanHelps is a peer-support and community platform. It is not an emergency response service or replacement for professional diagnosis or crisis care.

problem.statement: People dealing with emotional struggles often need community, structure, and trustworthy guidance, but current platforms are either too chaotic, too private, too hard to discover, or not designed for emotional support.

existingPlatformGaps.Reddit: Anonymous and discoverable, but chaotic and not designed for structured support.
existingPlatformGaps.Discord: Good for chat, but communities are hard to discover and can become fragmented.
existingPlatformGaps.FacebookGroups: Discoverable, but privacy and moderation quality can be poor.
existingPlatformGaps.WhatsApp: Good for small groups, but does not scale or support discovery.
existingPlatformGaps.Meetup: Good for events, but not designed around emotional support, small circles, or therapist-guided communities.

problem.solution: Create a focused platform with curated forums, separate structured support groups, therapist participation, meetings, reporting/moderation, and later AI-assisted safety and summaries.

beaner.meaning: Regular user seeking support or community.
canDo: Set and update a privacy-facing display name/alias
Reply to comments in nested discussion threads
Join or get matched into support groups
See persistent waitlist status and cancel a waitlist request
Use support group chat if they are an active group member
View and attend support group meetings if they are an active group member
Be attached to all featured forums conceptually
Access support group meetings/chat when assigned as group therapist
View therapist dashboard with assigned circles, upcoming sessions, recent messages, and basic weekly-insight placeholders
beanpist.verification: Therapist verification lives inside therapistProfile.verificationStatus. Admins can list pending therapists and update verification status through implemented backend routes. For the MVP, pending therapists may complete/update their profile, verified therapists can use therapist powers, and rejected therapists can log in but cannot resubmit or use therapist-only actions.

canDo: View grouped report summaries with report counts, reason breakdowns, high-priority flags, and auto-hidden flags
Verify, reject, or return therapist verification status to pending
admin.securityNote: Admin should not be available from public signup in production.

forumsAndSupportGroupsSeparated.oldIdea: Originally each forum had three layers: discussion, support groups, and forum-wide meetings.
forumsAndSupportGroupsSeparated.problemWithOldIdea: A support group could belong to many forums, which made ownership, discovery, and data modeling complicated.
forumsAndSupportGroupsSeparated.currentDecision: Forums and support groups are separate modules. Forums contain discussion and forum-wide meetings. Support groups live in a separate support system and use tags for filtering/matching.

forumsAreCurated.decision: Keep official featured forums curated by the platform for now.
forumsAreCurated.reasoning: Mental-health community spaces need moderation and structure. User-created forums can create duplicates and safety issues.
forumsAreCurated.futureOption: Users may later suggest forums through a ForumSuggestion flow for admin/therapist approval.

tagsAreFlexibleLayer.decision: Use broad forums plus specific tags.
tagsAreFlexibleLayer.why: Tags cover specific needs without creating a forum for every possible issue.

flow: Backend finds open/needs_members groups with highest tag overlap.
If a match exists, user joins best group.
If no group has space, user enters waitlist.
If enough similar waitlisted users exist, a new group is created.
When someone leaves a group, backend tries to fill the spot from the best waitlisted user.
If a waitlisted user manually joins or gets directly matched into a group, stale waiting entries are cancelled.
supportGroupMatching.mvpRule: Create a new group from waitlist when 6 similar users are available.

meetingsKeptSimpleForNow.decision: Support group meetings currently support simple one-time and basic recurring fields.
notHandledYet: Changing recurrence midway while preserving history
meetingsKeptSimpleForNow.futureArchitecture: Use SupportGroupMeetingSeries and SupportGroupMeetingOccurrence if advanced scheduling is needed.

therapistVerificationMvp.decision: Only verified beanpists can perform therapist-only actions. Pending beanpists can finish their profile while waiting for admin review. Rejected beanpists remain able to log in, but cannot resubmit their therapist profile in the MVP.
therapistOnlyActions: Use assigned-therapist access for support group meetings/chat
therapistVerificationMvp.frontendBehavior: Rejected beanpists are routed to the therapist home status screen instead of therapist onboarding.

privacyAliases.decision: Users have both an account name and a displayName. Community surfaces prefer displayName so beaners can participate without exposing their real/account name.
privacyAliases.safetyNote: Membership, reporting, waitlists, and auth use immutable user ids, so changing displayName does not allow duplicate group joins or repeated reports.

dailyKindnessQuotes.decision: Backend exposes a cached daily quote endpoint that uses ZenQuotes when available and beanHelps fallback quotes when unavailable.
dailyKindnessQuotes.frontendPlacement: Beaner home page Daily kindness card.
dailyKindnessQuotes.attribution: When ZenQuotes is used, frontend displays attribution text and link returned by the backend.

rootStructure.apps/api/models: Mongoose models used by API runtime.
rootStructure.apps/api/src/middleware: Auth, role authorization, validation, error handling.

importantEnv.apiClientUrl: CLIENT_URL should match the Vite frontend URL, usually http://localhost:5173

User.purpose: One auth model for beaners, beanpists, and admins.
User.note: displayName is the privacy-facing alias. Verification is therapist-only and belongs in therapistProfile.verificationStatus.

Forum.isActiveMeaning: Soft-hides/disables a forum without deleting its old posts, comments, or meetings.
Forum.isFeaturedMeaning: Marks official curated forums shown prominently and available to therapists by default.

ForumMembership.purpose: Tracks which beaner has joined which forum.
ForumMembership.note: Therapists conceptually see all featured forums and do not need manual memberships for MVP.

SupportGroup.purpose: Small support circle separate from forums.

statusMeaning.needs_members: Active but below minimumStartSize; should be refill-prioritized.
statusMeaning.open: Has enough members to operate and still has room.

SupportGroup.capacityPolicy: Default capacity is 8. Allowed range is 6-10. minimumStartSize defaults to 6.

SupportGroupMembership.purpose: One record per user per support group, tracking active/left membership and role.

SupportGroupWaitlist.purpose: Tracks users waiting for a support group match.
SupportGroupWaitlist.note: Matched and cancelled entries are kept for history/debugging instead of being deleted. Manual joins and direct auto-matches cancel stale waiting entries.

SupportGroupMeeting.purpose: Meetings scheduled by support group organizer/therapist/admin.

SupportGroupMessage.note: Realtime chat via Socket.IO can be added later on top of this model.

Report.purpose: Safety/moderation report against content or support groups.

actions.mark_reviewing: Admin has started reviewing the report.
actions.dismiss: Report reviewed and no action needed.
actions.resolve: Report handled without hiding the content.
actions.hide_content: Reported content hidden or support group closed.

Report.dedupeRule: One reporter can report a given target only once. Multiple different reporters can report the same target.
Report.priorityRule: Targets become high priority when report volume crosses the configured threshold or self-harm is reported.
Report.autoHideRule: Repeated serious reports can auto-hide content before admin review.

implementedOrStarted: Auth layout with beach background visual treatment
Signup page with account name, display name, role selection, and redirect behavior
Beaner onboarding page wired to backend
Therapist onboarding route guards rejected or already-completed profiles
Beaner home with joined forums, recommended forums auto-join, support circle/waitlist status, privacy alias editing, upcoming sessions, and daily kindness quote
Explore Forums with joined/explore states and join buttons
Forum detail with discussion and meetings layers
Forum posts with nested comment replies
Explore Support Groups with filters, auto-match, active-circle state, persistent waitlist state, leave current circle, and cancel waitlist
Support group detail with members, chat, prompts, resources, events, and reports
Report buttons and modal on posts, comments, support group messages, and support groups
Therapist home shows pending/rejected/verified verification states, assigned groups, upcoming sessions, recent messages, and insight metrics
Admin home, therapist verification page, and grouped reports page

routingDecision.afterLoginOrSignup: Frontend calls GET /api/auth/me then routes using getNextRoute(user).

frontendState.designDirection: The frontend currently uses a calm beach background, soft rounded cards, muted sage/teal accents, and accessible rounded typography. Earlier pixel theme experiments were replaced by a softer wellness theme.

safetyAndModeration.positioning: beanHelps should include safety disclaimers and should not be framed as crisis response.
currentModerationFlow: Any logged-in user can submit a report.
A single user cannot repeatedly report the same target.
Multiple different users can report the same target.
Admin can view grouped target summaries with report counts and reason breakdown.
Admin can apply actions such as hide_content.
Repeated serious reports can mark targets high priority and auto-hide content.

contentHidingBehavior.support_group: Set isActive = false and status = closed.

futureSafetyIdeas: Weekly AI summaries for therapist dashboards
Demo seed script for admins, therapists, beaners, forums, groups, messages, meetings, and reports

therapistDashboardTeammate.meaningOfAssignedSupportGroups: Support groups where SupportGroup.therapist equals the therapist user's id.

otherUsefulParallelTasks: Weekly AI summary generation and persistence

nextBackendSteps: Create an idempotent demo seed script for admin credentials, sample beaners, pending/verified/rejected therapists, forums, memberships, support groups, messages, meetings, waitlist entries, and reports.
Implement weekly AI summaries for therapist dashboards using recent support group messages, with a safe fallback when no AI key is configured.
Add API docs with request bodies for teammates.
Add production rate limits and stronger admin provisioning before any real-world use.
